title: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
author: Timothy Cole
orientation: Timothy Cole recounts his journey from junior-high awakening to societal evil, through years of pro-life involvement with pregnancy care centers and Kansans For Life, to a conversation with abolitionist Kevan Myers that convinced him to renounce pro-life incrementalism and publicly stand for the immediate abolition of abortion.
audiences: Pro-life movement volunteer or pregnancy center board member
Pastor evaluating church partnerships with pro-life organizations

questions.question: What first sparked Timothy Cole's lifelong concern about grave societal evil?
questions.answer: Learning in junior high history class about American slavery, the mistreatment of Native Americans, the Civil War, and the civil rights era shattered his assumption that grave evil was confined to ancient times and motivated him to fight the evils of his own age.
questions.quote: Learning of our nation’s history of slavery, mistreatment of Native Americans, the Civil War, the civil rights era etc. disturbed me to my core and removed my imagined barrier of time between then and now, taking some of my innocence.

questions.question: What pro-life paradigm did Cole encounter while serving on a pregnancy care center board?
questions.audience: Pro-life movement volunteer or pregnancy center board member
questions.answer: He was taught that women are the 'second victim' of abortion, that pro-life laws exempt mothers from prosecution, and that abortion is openly discussed with mothers at the center 'because it is an option.'
questions.quote: For example: women are the “second victim” of abortion, pro-life laws exempt women from prosecution, and abortion is an option discussed with mothers at pregnancy care centers “because it is an option.”

questions.question: What moment at the National Right to Life conference in Overland Park first jolted Cole?
questions.answer: A speaker openly endorsed legislation containing rape, incest, and life-of-the-mother exceptions, which Cole understood as supporting the murder of certain babies; he left the workshop confused and in tears.
questions.quote: I nearly had to pick my jaw up off the floor when a speaker said they support legislation with exceptions that allow for the murder of babies conceived in rape, incest and for danger to the life of the mother.

questions.question: Why did Cole stay in the pro-life establishment despite his misgivings?
questions.audience: Pro-life movement volunteer or pregnancy center board member
questions.answer: He was afraid of losing the only political outlet he knew of for fighting abortion and was persuaded by appeals to political realities and the supposed need to change hearts and minds before demanding justice.
questions.quote: Probably out of a desire to not lose the only anti-abortion movement I was aware of, I too easily was talked back into the Pro-Life Establishment by assertions that we must do what we can given political realities, and that we must change hearts and minds before we can demand justice for the preborn.

questions.question: What did Kansans For Life trainers tell Cole about their legislative strategy?
questions.answer: Trainers told him immediate ending of abortion was impossible, so the focus had to be on restricting abortion however possible, and that even failed pro-life measures were crafted to frame the debate and shift public opinion.
questions.quote: I was told that even if the pro-life measures do not pass, they are crafted to frame the debate in a way that sways public opinion toward pro-life thinking.

questions.question: How did a fellow Christian react to Kevan Myers' uncompromising no-abortion position?
questions.audience: Pastor evaluating church partnerships with pro-life organizations
questions.answer: The brother repeatedly warned Cole that Kevan was 'really extreme' for opposing all abortion, leaving Cole baffled that any serious Christian would consider 'no abortion' to be an extreme stance.
questions.quote: “Kevan is really extreme on abortion… He is for no abortion… I mean he is for no abortion, NO abortion!”

questions.question: What did Cole find missing from the Kansans For Life Value Them Both presentation?
questions.audience: Pastor evaluating church partnerships with pro-life organizations
questions.answer: The slides focused on parental notification, pre-abortion counseling, informed consent, and ultrasound access before a baby is killed, not on actually ending abortion, prompting Cole to shout that the presentation had nothing to do with ending abortion.
questions.quote: I became increasingly frustrated and eventually got mad and shouted, “This presentation has nothing to do with ending abortion!”

questions.question: How did Cole's pre-engagement phone call with Kevan Myers turn out?
questions.answer: Cole had planned to persuade Kevan that incremental bills were a path toward abolition, but Kevan walked him through scriptures on justice, impartiality, and the law as a tutor, and Cole ended the call as the one who had been persuaded.
questions.quote: I’d planned to persuade and was the one being persuaded.

questions.question: What scriptural framework finally convinced Cole that abolitionism was right?
questions.answer: Kevan walked him through passages on justice, refusing to show favoritism, and the function of the law as a tutor that teaches God's standards, leading Cole to see that scripture prescribes abolitionism in response to societal sin.
questions.quote: Kevan walked me through scriptures about justice, not showing favoritism, and how the law is a tutor.

questions.question: What concrete steps did Cole take after deciding to become an abolitionist?
questions.answer: He cancelled his Kansans For Life speaking engagement and called his pastor to alert him that his planned introduction of the Value Them Both amendment to the congregation would instead be a public argument against it.
questions.quote: I cancelled my KFL speaking engagement and called my pastor to alert him that my introduction to the congregation of the Value Them Both amendment (the next morning) would be to speak against it.

questions.question: How does Cole respond to the worry that turning pro-lifers into abolitionists leaves them with nothing to do about abortion?
questions.audience: Pastor evaluating church partnerships with pro-life organizations
questions.answer: He shared that concern at the time but has since found many opportunities to work toward abolition alongside Kevan's team in Kansas City and the folks at Abortion is Murder Kansas.
questions.quote: I shared his concern at the time, but have since found many opportunities to work toward abolition in tandem with abolitionists in our area such as Kevan’s team in Kansas City, and the folks at [Abortion is Murder Kansas](https://aimks.org/).

questions.question: What does Cole say pro-life legislation has actually taught Christians and the broader culture?
questions.answer: By protecting some babies and not others on arbitrary criteria, pro-life laws and rhetoric teach a warped view of justice, deceive secular culture, and poison believers' minds with what amounts to pro-abortion thinking.
questions.quote: Pro-life efforts, with my support, have deceived the secular culture and poisoned the minds of believers with nothing less than pro-abortion thinking.

questions.question: What lesson did Cole draw from sharing the Gospel with a father at Planned Parenthood?
questions.answer: Even after acknowledging the act was murder, the father chose to kill his baby for emotional reasons, an interaction that left Cole more passionately opposed to abortion and to any worldly philosophy that rejects the biblical mandate of immediate repentance.
questions.quote: interactions like this which expose the evil of abortion in all its horror one make me more passionately despise abortion and any worldly philosophy that rejects the Biblical mandate of demanding our state and nation immediately repent for the sin of abortion.

questions.question: How does Cole use Presbyterian abolitionist George Bourne to close his argument?
questions.answer: He cites Bourne's claim that the system of slavery admitted no cure but total and immediate abolition, since gradual emancipation is itself a recognition that the practice is rightful, drawing the parallel to abortion today.
questions.quote: The system is so entirely corrupt that it admits of no cure but a TOTAL AND IMMEDIATE abolition.  For a gradual emancipation is a virtual recognition of the right, and establishes the rectitude of the practice.

related.title: My Pro-Life-to-Abolitionist Conversion
related.reason: Another first-person conversion narrative from pro-life involvement to abolitionism, providing a parallel testimony to Cole's.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Systematizes the objections Cole came to hold about incremental legislation like Value Them Both.

related.title: Incrementalism Versus Immediatism in a Nutshell
related.reason: Frames the same incrementalist-vs-immediatist debate Cole personally resolved through his conversation with Kevan Myers.

related.title: The Holiness of God Destroys Pro-Life Incrementalism
related.reason: Develops the biblical and theological case Cole found in scripture for refusing pro-life compromise.

related.title: No Exceptions
related.reason: Spells out the principled abolitionist refusal of rape, incest, and life-of-the-mother exceptions that first shocked Cole at the National Right to Life conference.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Articulates the categorical distinction between the two movements that Cole crossed in his conversion.

related.title: Refuting Toby Sumpter's "Eight Tenets of Smashmouth Incrementalism"
related.reason: Engages directly with the kind of incrementalist apologia that almost kept Cole inside Kansans For Life.
